quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 8
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A cargo transport plane landing on a short runway touches down with an initial forward speed of $68.0\text{ m/s}$ and immediately deploys thrust reversers. If the plane comes to a complete stop over a straight distance of $520\text{ m}$, calculate the magnitude of its uniform deceleration.

A chemical engineer calculates the volume and surface area profiles for a series of storage tanks. Convert a surface area reading of $0.0540\text{ cm}^2$ directly into square millimeters ($\text{mm}^2$).

A heavy metal crate is pulled along a rough horizontal warehouse floor by a rope that forms an upward angle of $25.0^{\circ}$ relative to the ground. Analyze the work done on the crate by the normal force exerted upward by the floor during a horizontal displacement.
questions.explanation: The normal force acts vertically upward while displacement is horizontal. Force and displacement are perpendicular — zero work.

questions.question: A heavy vertical stamping machine uses an internal spring mechanism to rebound its die plate. The spring has a stiffness constant of $1.65 \times 10^5\text{ N/m}$ and stores exactly $330\text{ J}$ of elastic strain energy when fully compressed. Determine the distance the spring was compressed from its natural state.

A $0.420\text{ kg}$ test projectile is fired horizontally into a thick block of ballistic gelatin at $45.0\text{ m/s}$. The gelatin exerts a constant average resistive force of $1,350\text{ N}$ to stop the projectile. Calculate the total depth of penetration into the block.

A $0.0570\text{ kg}$ mass moving horizontally at $26.0\text{ m/s}$ is struck by a mechanical paddle, which reverses its direction and sends it backward at $34.0\text{ m/s}$. If the interaction lasts for $0.00240\text{ s}$, calculate the average net force exerted on the mass by the paddle.

A high-pressure industrial pump lifts $165\text{ kg}$ of liquid chemicals vertically through a pipe system to a storage vat $8.40\text{ m}$ above the intake valve. This lifting operation is completed at a steady rate in exactly $12.0\text{ s}$. Determine the average useful power output delivered by the pump. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

An electric ventilation motor draws $3.60 \times 10^4\text{ J}$ of electrical energy during a short operational cycle. The motor has an efficiency rating of $68.5\%$. Calculate the total amount of useful mechanical work output it performs.

An unstable $8.50\text{ kg}$ mechanical component at rest on a frictionless test bench suddenly ruptures into two segments due to internal pressure. A $5.80\text{ kg}$ segment is propelled due east at $14.0\text{ m/s}$. Calculate the velocity of the second segment ($2.70\text{ kg}$) immediately following the rupture.

A rigging crew uses a third-class lever tool to position a heavy machine housing. The distance from the pivot to the effort force is $0.350\text{ m}$, while the distance from the fulcrum to the output load is $1.40\text{ m}$. Calculate the ideal mechanical advantage (IMA) of this lever configuration.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on spring energy, third-class levers, and force during rebounds.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
